grainFreeDryFood.name: Canagan Free-Run Chicken
grainFreeDryFood.mainIngredient: Freshly prepared deboned chicken (26%), dried chicken (25%)
grainFreeDryFood.summary: Premium British-made grain-free kibble with 60% chicken content. Includes joint-supporting glucosamine and MSM, plus sweet potato for slow-release energy. One of the most popular grain-free options in the UK.
pros: Sweet potato instead of white potato
cons: Some dogs dislike the small kibble size

grainFreeDryFood.name: Orijen Original
grainFreeDryFood.mainIngredient: Fresh chicken meat (13%), fresh turkey meat (7%), fresh whole eggs (7%)
grainFreeDryFood.summary: The gold standard of grain-free dog food. 85% quality animal ingredients with multiple protein sources mimicking a natural diet. WholePrey ratios include organs and cartilage for complete nutrition without synthetic supplements.
pros: 85% animal ingredients — highest on this list
WholePrey ratios include organs for natural nutrition
No synthetic amino acid supplements needed
cons: Rich formula may cause loose stools initially
Strong fish smell some owners dislike

grainFreeDryFood.name: Lily's Kitchen Chicken & Duck
grainFreeDryFood.mainIngredient: Freshly prepared chicken (25%), freshly prepared duck (10%)
grainFreeDryFood.summary: Well-known premium British brand offering natural grain-free recipes with 50% freshly prepared meat. Certified B Corp with ethical sourcing. Popular choice with good availability in supermarkets and pet shops.
pros: No artificial preservatives, fillers, or derivatives
Good range of flavours and sizes
cons: Premium price for the meat percentage

grainFreeDryFood.name: Symply Fresh Turkey
grainFreeDryFood.summary: Outstanding value grain-free option from a well-regarded UK brand. Uses freshly prepared turkey as the sole protein — ideal for dogs with chicken sensitivities. Sweet potato and pea-based carbohydrates.
pros: Single protein source — great for allergies

grainFreeDryFood.name: Eden Holistic Original Cuisine
grainFreeDryFood.mainIngredient: Freshly prepared chicken (30%), freshly prepared salmon (14%), dried chicken (12%)
grainFreeDryFood.summary: Exceptional 80/20 formula from a small-batch UK manufacturer. Gently prepared at low temperatures to preserve nutrients. Six animal proteins in one recipe for a varied, biologically appropriate diet.
cons: Multiple proteins = not ideal for elimination diets

grainFreeDryFood.name: Harringtons Grain Free Turkey & Veg
grainFreeDryFood.summary: The most affordable grain-free option in the UK, widely available in supermarkets. Good entry point for dogs transitioning to grain-free. Lower meat content than premium options but genuine value for money.
pros: Available everywhere — Tesco, Asda, Pets at Home
No artificial colours, flavours, or preservatives
cons: Only 30% meat — lowest on our list

grainFreeDryFood.name: Forthglade Natural Lifestage Chicken
grainFreeDryFood.summary: Devon-based family brand making natural dog food since 1971. Grain-free cold-pressed option that's gentle on digestion. Known for excellent palatability and steady energy release.
pros: Steady energy release throughout the day
cons: Cold-pressed kibble different texture — some dogs take time to adjust

grainFreeDryFood.name: Acana Classics Prairie Poultry
grainFreeDryFood.mainIngredient: Fresh chicken (12%), fresh turkey (4%), chicken meal (12%)
grainFreeDryFood.summary: From the same makers as Orijen but at a more accessible price point. 60% quality poultry ingredients with 40% fruit, vegetables, and botanicals. A solid mid-range option that delivers excellent nutrition.
pros: Same manufacturer as Orijen — trusted quality
Good balance of quality and price
cons: Not as premium as Orijen sibling
Can be harder to find in shops

grainFreeDryFood.name: Millie's Wolfheart Riverside Mix
grainFreeDryFood.mainIngredient: Duck (40% — freshly prepared duck 26%, dried duck 14%), trout (20%)
grainFreeDryFood.summary: The UK dog forum favourite. Millie's Wolfheart is a family-run brand that's built a cult following among working dog owners and allergy-conscious pet parents. Every recipe is formulated without gluten, grain, rice, chicken, eggs, beet pulp, or synthetic additives. Riverside Mix uses duck and trout — both novel proteins that are excellent for dogs with common food sensitivities.
pros: No chicken, grain, egg, or rice — truly hypoallergenic
Excellent value at £5.08/kg for premium quality
Massive UK forum following — consistently top-recommended
cons: Primarily sold direct — limited on Amazon/shops
Strong fish smell from trout content
Not widely stocked in high-street pet shops

grainFreeDryFood.name: Scrumbles Salmon Grain-Free
grainFreeDryFood.mainIngredient: Salmon (30% — freshly prepared salmon 20%, dried salmon 10%)
grainFreeDryFood.summary: The gut-health specialist. Scrumbles is a rising UK brand that appeared on Dragon's Den and has since become a staple in Pets at Home and major supermarkets. Every recipe includes added probiotics (1 billion live bacteria per serving) and slippery elm for digestive support. Their grain-free salmon recipe is specifically designed for dogs with sensitive stomachs.
pros: Added probiotics — 1 billion live bacteria per serving
Widely available — Pets at Home, Sainsbury's, Ocado
Free from common allergens (grain, gluten, soy, dairy, egg)
Dragon's Den backed — strong UK brand
cons: 50% meat content — lower than ultra-premium brands
Contains potato and sweet potato as carb sources

grainFreeDryFood.name: Pooch & Mutt Skin & Coat
grainFreeDryFood.mainIngredient: Herring (24% — dried herring 14%, freshly prepared herring 10%), linseed
grainFreeDryFood.summary: Purpose-built for dogs with skin issues. Pooch & Mutt's Skin & Coat formula uses herring as its primary protein — rich in omega-3 fatty acids that directly support skin health and coat condition. Added linseed provides further omega-3, while the grain-free recipe removes common inflammatory triggers. Vet-recommended and widely available.
pros: Specifically formulated for skin and coat health
Available on Amazon, Pets at Home, and direct
cons: 45% meat content — lower end of the spectrum
Not a single-protein food (contains some chicken liver)
